content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF

DAVIS, CRAIG & YOUNG

OFFICES OF
H. THEODORE CRAIG III, INC.
SAMUEL P. YOUNG, INC.
DANIEL E. CUMMINS, INC.
JAMES H. FRENCH
ROBERT J. DAVIS
WILLIAM J. BRUTOCAO
JOHN BARNARD

PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

1134 BALLENA BOULEVARD
POST OFFICE BOX 2426
ALAMEDA, CALIFORNIA 94501

TELEPHONE
(415) 521-1211
IN REPLY PLEASE
REFER TO FILE

February 27, 1979

John Giansello
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 94814

Re: Doric Development, Inc.

Dear John:

This letter is a confirmation of our telephone conversation
today wherein we discussed the FPPC reporting requirements
for a major donor committee who conducts a political
survey and makes the results of the survey known to
political candidates. As you know, this firm represents
Doric Development, Inc., a company which has in prior
years filed statements as a major donor committee. At the
present time, I do not know whether Doric has reached
the $5,000 threshold to make it a major donor committee
for the present year.

A particular area of concern which we have been discussing
these past few days involves a telephone survey taken in
the City of Alameda on February 17 and 18, 1979, regarding
upcoming municipal elections. The estimated cost of the
survey is $2,600, although a final bill has not yet been
received. The survey was a scientifically reliable and
neutral poll of a random sample of Alameda residents.

The results of the survey have been supplied to the local
newspaper as well as to each of the seven (7) candidates
named in the survey. Based on these facts, you have
advised me that Doric's reporting obligations are as
follows:

(1) Assuming that Doric meets the $5,000 major donor
threshold, Doric must report the total cost of the survey
along with a list of the candidates to whom the results
were given.

(2) Doric should advise each candidate to whom the
results were supplied that each candidate will have a

John Giansello
February 27, 1979
Page Two


reporting obligation if he uses the results of the survey
to further his campaign, for example, through advertising,
soliciting contributions, or formulating campaign strategy.
If, by so doing, a candidate incurs a reporting obligation,
the particular candidate must report the survey as a
non-monetary receipt at a value equal to the total cost of
the survey divided by the number of candidates who received
copies of the results. (At present, this figure is
expected to be $2,800 divided by 7, i.e., $400.) Thus,
Doric must tell each candidate the total cost of the
survey and the number of candidates to whom survey results
were supplied.

(3) If the survey results were supplied only to a newspaper
or other news media, then Doric would incur no reporting
obligations.

(4) Since the above calculation shows a $400 contribution
to each candidate, Doric has an obligation to file an
abbreviated statement in connection with the upcoming
local election under Government Code Section 84204.1 if
Doric meets the $5,000 major donor threshold within the
time period in which abbreviated statements must be
filed.

I trust that the foregoing represents an accurate
recapitulation of our conversation. Doric intends to rely
on the advice contained in this letter derived from my
conversations with you. Accordingly, if you disagree in
any manner with the contents of this letter, please notify
Ted Craig or Susan Hoosier of this office immediately.

Thank you for your assistance and cooperation.

Very truly yours,

DAVIS, CRAIG & YOUNG

By [signature]
WILLIAM J./BRUTOCAO

WJB/hk
cc: Doric Development, Inc.

LAW OFFICES OF

JAMES B. DAVIS, INC.
H. THEODORE CRAIG III, INC.
WILLIAM J. YOUNG
DANIEL E. CUMMINS, INC.
JAMES H. FRENCH
PATRICK J. MALONEY
WILLIAM J. BRUTOCAO
ALAN J. LYONS

DAVIS, CRAIG & YOUNG
PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

1134 BALLENA BOULEVARD
POST OFFICE BOX 2426
ALAMEDA, CALIFORNIA 94501

TELEPHONE
(415) 521-1211

IN REPLY PLEASE
REFER TO FILE #

March 14, 1979


Mr. John Giansello
Fair Political Practices Committee
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 94814

Re: Harbor Bay Isle Associates

Dear Mr. Giansello:

Please be advised that our letter to you of February 27, 1979
was inaccurate in that Harbor Bay Isle Associates was the
contributor of the funds used to obtain the survey for the
municipal elections. We apologize for any confusion that this
may have caused you. It is our understanding, however, that
the information provided in our letter of February 27, 1979
to Doric Development would also be applicable to Harbor Bay
Isle Associates. Therefore, we have advised HBIA to follow
the procedures outlined in William Brutocao's letter and would
appreciate it if you would advise us in the event our under-
standing of this matter is inconsistent with the requirements.

Thank you for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

DAVIS, CRAIG & YOUNG

By H. THEODORE CRAIG III

HTC:smm

cc: Ronald H. Cowan
    Kent Hockabout

[Handwritten notes at bottom - illegible]
